messages.content: The problem is that if a player from the second TrainingArea goes to the same corner that has food in the first TrainingArea, then the food will disappear in the first TrainingArea.

Your understanding is correct. The issue is that the food objects are spawned with the global world position, which might cause interference between different training areas. To fix this, change all positions to be local to each TrainingArea.

1. In the `TrainingArea` script, replace the line in the `SpawnFoods()` method:
```csharp
GameObject foodInstance = Instantiate(foodPrefab, spawnPosition, Quaternion.identity, transform);
```
with:
```csharp
GameObject foodInstance = Instantiate(foodPrefab, spawnPosition + transform.position, Quaternion.identity, transform);
```
This makes sure that food instances are spawned with local coordinates relative to their respective TrainingArea.

2. Also, in the `EatFood()` method, change this line:
```csharp
food.transform.position = Vector3.one * 9999;
```
to:
```csharp
food.transform.localPosition = Vector3.one * 9999;
```
This will ensure that the food object is moved far away within the local space of the TrainingArea and prevents interference between different instances.

With these changes, food objects in different TrainingArea instances should not interfere with each other, ensuring that the food does not disappear unexpectedly.

you did not understand me. even if there is only one TrainingArea left on the stage, and if I move it to the right, my food will be considered eaten if it is far to the right. The same for the left, the more I move the TrainingArea to the left, the more it will be on the left, even if the sprites are displayed correctly. The same goes for top and bottom. The ideal position of the TrainingArea is 0,0,0 where everything works correctly. Fix it.

The issue occurs because, in the `ResetFoods()` method, you're calculating the spawn position using local coordinates but calling `Instantiate()` with world coordinates. You should convert the spawn position to world coordinates for each food object when creating them.

Modify the line inside the `foreach` loop in the `ResetFoods()` method:

```csharp
GameObject foodInstance = Instantiate(foodPrefab, spawnPosition + transform.position, Quaternion.identity, transform);
```

This line ensures that the food objects are instantiated based on the world coordinates of their respective TrainingAreas.

By making this change to the `ResetFoods()` method, the food should appear in the appropriate TrainingArea after an episode ends.
